# CANDIDATE: BEARISH_REJECTION_DELAYED_ENTRY_2BARS

**Filed:** 2026-06-18  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** filter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis
Waiting for 2 bars after the BEARISH_REJECTION trigger (post-confirmation) may filter out false signals and improve the edge by avoiding whipsaws, especially on loser days, while capturing sufficient profit on winner days by entering after the initial noise.

## Mechanism
Current engine enters on the trigger bar (the bar where at least 2 of 3 BEARISH_REJECTION conditions are met: level rejection, EMA ribbon flip, confluence). 
Proposed engine waits for 2 additional bars after the trigger bar (i.e., skips the trigger bar and the next bar, then enters on the second bar after the trigger bar closes). 
All other aspects of the BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON setup (context filters, contract selection, stop, target, management) remain unchanged.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** Testing across J's 7 anchor days (3 winners, 4 losers) - small sample (N=7) with high overfit risk; selection method fixed by OP-16 (immutable source-of-truth trades)
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no OOS test performed; proposal based solely on anchor days)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (no real OPRA fill validation on top 3 J days)
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: Delayed entry misses entire move on winner day, turning winner to loser
   - Max drawdown: Late entry on loser days could increase losses if move continues against us
   - Blow-up scenario: Market gaps against us after delay, entering at worse price with larger losses
6. **Concentration:** Cannot state concentration without P&L distribution; if delayed entry only works on subset of anchor days, concentration could be high

## Pre-merge gate
Stage-1 backtest on full 16-month dataset for J's anchor days edge_capture; walk-forward OOS validation; real-fills validation on top 3 J anchor days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04); gym validators (67/67 PASS)

## Confidence
3 / 10 -- Low confidence due to zero supporting data; hypothesis intuitive but untested on anchor days

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact
No direct conflict with existing candidates; modifies BEARISH_REJECTION entry timing independently of time-of-day gates or watcher proposals. May complement BEARISH_REJECTION_MORNING watcher (rank 20) if delayed entry still falls within 09:35-10:55 ET window, but requires validation. No overlap with V14E_BEAR_TIME_OF_DAY_GATE (rank 17) as mechanism differs (time filter vs entry delay).
